title: senegalia cinerea
intro: Senegalia cinerea, also known as the grey thorn, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to the savannas and open woodlands of southern Africa, where it is often found on rocky hillsides and in dry, sandy soils. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving pain. Herbal preparations of Senegalia cinerea include infusions and decoctions, which can be made by steeping the leaves and branches in hot water, and are used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory infections and skin conditions.
